title: The Japanese artists I’ve been listening to lately.
intro: This is a small, modular archive of the artists, albums, and songs I keep returning to. I kept the tone close to the draft, but shaped it into a cleaner website with cards, hover details, and linked artist pages.

cards.title: A Son of the Sun
cards.subtitle: Uyama Hiroto
cards.note: A warm, lyrical record full of beautiful acoustic songs and a really strong sense of atmosphere.

cards.title: R4 - Ridge Racer Type 4
cards.subtitle: Ridge Racer Series
cards.note: An energetic, stylish set that makes the whole franchise feel unforgettable.

cards.title: Adol Christin - Ys 35th Anniversary
cards.subtitle: Falcom Sound Team jdk
cards.note: A very recent discovery, but the music already feels broad and deeply rewarding.

cards.title: Castlevania: Curse of Darkness
cards.subtitle: Castlevania Sound Team / Michiru Yamane
cards.note: The soundtrack that makes the whole series feel even more vivid and personal.

cards.title: Spectrum
cards.subtitle: Spectrum
cards.note: A wider, brass-forward sound that still lands on melody and momentum.

cards.title: Casiopea
cards.subtitle: Casiopea
cards.note: The album that made the whole fusion-jazz lane feel immediate.

cards.title: Modal Soul
cards.subtitle: Nujabes
cards.note: A calm, reflective record that still feels huge in the room.

cards.title: Steppin’
cards.subtitle: Kangaroo
cards.note: A bright, swinging record with too many great songs to ignore.
